title: Vill
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Youmay|the [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone trooper]]|[[Vill Goir]], [[Dark side adept/Legends|dark side adept]]}} {{Character |type=Galactic Empire |image=[[File:Vill.jpg]] |name=Vill |homeworld=[[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]]{{DB|organization|clonetroopers|clone troopers}} |birth= |death=[[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]] (16:9), [[Bandomeer/Legends|Bandomeer]][[Dark Times 15|''Dark Times'' 15]] |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]] {{C|[[Cloning/Legends|clone]]}} |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]][[Dark Times 1|''Dark Times'' 1]] |pronouns=He/him{{CSWECite|III|306|Vill, Commanderr}} |donor=[[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] |height=1.83 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] |mass= |hair=Black''[[Star Wars: Dark Times: The Path to Nowhere]]'' |feathers= |eyes=Brown |skin= |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] **[[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] *[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] **[[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|We live to serve, but a soldier needs to feel useful.|Commander Vill|}} '''Vill''' was a [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] of the [[Death/Legends|deceased]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]]. Originally a [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone trooper]] of the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], Vill became one of the [[Clone stormtrooper|first]] [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] after the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] was reorganized into the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. As a clone programmed with absolute loyalty, Vill was an obedient [[Soldier/Legends|soldier]] who executed orders without moral or ethical restraint. A month after [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] [[Declaration of a New Order|Declaration of a]] [[New Order/Legends|New Order]], Vill served as [[Clone Commander/Legends|commander]] of a [[Regiment/Legends|regiment]] of stormtroopers from the [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]], an elite unit of clone soldiers who served directly under the command of the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]. Vill and his regiment were dispatched on a [[Cleansing of New Plympto|mission]] to [[New Plympto/Legends|New Plympto]] with orders to eradicate the last remaining pockets of [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist]] resistance. ==Biography== ===Battle of New Plympto=== {{Quote|It's ''over'', my Lord. The Separatist holdouts have been destroyed. No survivors. They fought to the last being.|Commander Vill|}} In [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], a month after [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] [[Declaration of a New Order|abolished]] the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] in order to create the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]],{{Film|III}} Vill was one of millions of former [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] who continued service as a [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]]. By this time, he was a [[Regiment/Legends|regiment]] [[Clone Commander/Legends|commander]] in the elite [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]], and thus he answered directly to the Emperor's second-in-command, the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]].[[Dark Times 2|''Dark Times'' 2]] [[File:Vill_Vader.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Commander Vill delivers news of the massacre of the Nosaurians to Darth Vader.]] Around the same time, the [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial]] [[Cleansing of New Plympto|subjugation]] of [[New Plympto/Legends|New Plympto]] proceeded at a slow pace with great difficulty. The native [[Nosaurian/Legends|Nosaurians]], former members of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], created their own [[New Plympto resistance (Imperial Period)|resistance cell]] after the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] was concluded. Ironically, they found a new ally in a former enemy. [[Jedi General/Legends|General]] [[Dass Jennir]], a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] survivor of [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]], joined the Nosaurians' fight against the Empire. As a result of the Nosaurians' ongoing rebellion, Emperor Palpatine dispatched a stormtrooper regiment of the 501st Legion, under the leadership of Commander Vill, to New Plympto with orders to bring the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] under Imperial control by annihilating the rebels. After their arrival, Vill and his clone stormtroopers steadily pushed the Nosaurians back until the rebels were forced to make a final stand at [[Half-Axe Pass]]. The [[Cleansing of New Plympto|Battle of Half-Axes Pass]] was a complete massacre in which the Nosaurian army was almost utterly destroyed. Those who surrendered after the battle were summarily executed by the 501st Legion. Their families were captured and, by order of the Emperor, Commander Vill had them all processed into [[Slavery/Legends|slave market]] on [[Orvax IV]]. Afterward, the clone commander reported the details of the battle to Darth Vader via [[Holocomm/Legends|holocomm]]. As the 501st prepared to depart the conquered planet of New Plympto, Commander Vill returned to Vader's [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperator''-class]] [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] ''[[Exactor/Legends|Exactor]]''. He reported directly to Commander [[Appo/Legends|Appo]], Vader's second-in-command in the 501st Legion, who wanted to know about an infiltration incident that occurred after the battle. Vill, unaware that the infiltrator was the Jedi Dass Jennir, said that an [[Alpha-3 Nimbus-class V-wing starfighter/Legends|Alpha-3 ''Nimbus''-class V-wing]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] was stolen from the [[203rd Division]] and used to create a distraction that enabled most of the grounded civilian [[Starship/Legends|ships]] to escape from New Plympto.[[Dark Times 4|''Dark Times'' 4]] ===Death=== {{Quote|Commander Vill, I have a question. I require a ''truthful'' answer."
"Uh, of course, sir.|Darth Vader and Commander Vill|}} [[File:Appo_and_Vill.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Commanders Appo and Vill of the Imperial 501st Legion]] Three [[Standard month/Legends|months]] later, Commander Vill and the 501st Legion participated in the Imperial [[Battle of Bandomeer|conquest]] of the planet [[Bandomeer/Legends|Bandomeer]], an operation that was personally overseen by Darth Vader. In the aftermath of the campaign, which was easily won against a group of lightly-armed rebellious [[Miner/Legends|miners]], Vill provided a casualty report to the Sith Lord. Vader then took the opportunity to ask Vill if the clone stormtroopers were indoctrinated with a [[Contingency Orders for the Grand Army of the Republic: Order Initiation, Orders 1 Through 150|contingency order]] to kill him; an order that they would carry out without question if it came from Emperor Palpatine. Vill's hesitation to answer the question confirmed Vader's suspicion. He then [[The Force/Legends|Force]] [[Telekinesis/Legends|pushed]] the clone commander off of a cliff to ensure that his inquiry did not reach the Emperor. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|I was concerned we'd end up as glorified policemen. It's good to have a mission.|Commander Vill|}} [[File:MassacreofNewPlympto.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Commander Vill and his 501st stormtroopers executed their Nosaurian prisoners without hesitation.]] Like virtually all [[Cloning/Legends|clones]] who were grown to serve the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]],{{Film|II}} Vill was programmed with absolute loyalty to the command hierarchy. As such, his full allegiance belonged to [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], the [[Supreme Commander (Republic)|Commander-in-Chief]] of the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]]. When Palpatine [[Declaration of a New Order|declared]] himself [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|emperor]] of the first [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], Vill dedicated his existence to enforcing Palpatine's [[New Order/Legends|New Order]] over the [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]. As typical of a clone of [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]], Vill's programmed loyalty made him susceptible to following any order without question or moral regard. He was neither conflicted by the ethical dilemma of executing unarmed prisoners, nor bothered by the transporting of civilian families into [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]]. On the contrary, he adapted quickly to his new life as a [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]]. During the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], Vill was concerned that the clones would be diminished as "glorified [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|policemen]]" after the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatists]] were defeated. Since the [[Rise of the Empire era|rise]] of the Empire necessitated further military operations, Vill was very pleased by the fact that the clones were still being used as [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]]. Furthermore, Vill fully accepted the purpose and role of all [[Clone stormtrooper|cloned stormtroopers]]; they existed only to serve the Emperor until [[Death/Legends|death]]. He felt empathy for his fellow clones to a certain extent, and believed that soldiers had to feel relevant—if only for the sake of [[Trooper/Legends|troop]] morale. In terms of physical appearance, Vill bore a basic resemblance to millions of other clones from the same [[Clone template/Legends|genetic template]]; a genetic result of being a clone of [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]], a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] who was killed during the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|first battle]] of the Clone Wars. By the time he and his [[Regiment/Legends|regiment]] were sent to [[New Plympto/Legends|New Plympto]], Vill's "face"—or more accurately, Jango Fett's face—sustained several scars. As was the same case with his cloned "brethren," Vill was also designed to age at a faster rate than real [[Human/Legends|Human]] beings. Thus, his physical age was at least twice as long as his actual, chronological age. Ironically, his loyalty to Emperor Palpatine was the cause of his demise. As a [[Clone Commander/Legends|commander]] in the [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]], Vill was loyal to his superior officer, the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]. But when Vader inquired about a secret contingency order to eliminate him, Vill was incapable of complying with an answer. His loyalty to Vader conflicted with his allegiance to Palpatine. Yet his lack of a reply was all the confirmation that Vader required before he killed the clone who had become nothing more than a loose end. ==Equipment== When [[Clone Commander/Legends|Commander]] Vill became an [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial]] [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]], he continued to use his [[Phase II clone trooper armor/Legends|Phase II battle armor]], which originally served as the armored uniform of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]]. Vill's armor sported the distinctive [[blue]] markings that identified him as a member of the [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]]. During the [[Cleansing of New Plympto|conquest of]] [[New Plympto/Legends|New Plympto]], his armor was modified with the addition of a [[Pauldron/Legends|pauldron]] and [[Kama/Legends|kama]]. In addition to the [[DC-15A blaster rifle/Legends|DC-15A blaster rifle]], the primary weapon of [[Cloning/Legends|cloned]] [[Clone stormtrooper|stormtroopers]] at the time, Vill was also equipped with dual [[DC-17 hand blaster/Legends|DC-17 hand blasters]]. Three months after the Battle of New Plympto, the Imperial code of uniformity became more strictly enforced, and thus the [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] were required to remove the color designs on their armor. Vill obliged the new custom and restored his armor to its full [[white]] appearance. He also discarded his pauldron and kama, and thus adopted the same appearance as the other rank-and-file stormtroopers. ==Appearances== *[[Dark Times 1|''Dark Times'' 1]] {{1st}} *[[Dark Times 2|''Dark Times'' 2]] *[[Dark Times 3|''Dark Times'' 3]] {{Mo}} *[[Dark Times 4|''Dark Times'' 4]] *[[Dark Times 15|''Dark Times'' 15]] ==Sources== *{{HasbroCite|set=Star Wars: 30th Anniversary Collection|text=''Star Wars: Order 66'' (Series 2)|pack=#4: Emperor Palpatine & Commander Vill|altlink=https://www.rebelscum.com/TACorder66-4.asp}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Vill |es=Vill |pl=Vill }} [[Category:Clone stormtrooper officers of the Galactic Empire]] [[Category:Clone trooper commanders]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Officers of the 501st Legion (Galactic Empire)]]
